reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: worker.js loads REAL transformers.js translation pipelines (pipeline('translation', 'Xenova/opus-mt-<pair>', {device:'wasm', dtype:'q8'})) and calls pipe(text, {num_beams, max_new_tokens:512}) on-device. Unlike a big multilingual model, MarianMT/OPUS-MT is BILINGUAL: one small (~105 MB q8) encoder-decoder per language pair; the worker holds a pair→pipeline Map and loads each on demand. Streaming via TextStreamer for greedy decode. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean (console-clean) tested pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. No measured latency fabricated.
rubric.notes: Because the pair IS the model, there are no src_lang/tgt_lang args — a genuine architectural contrast with the M2M100/NLLB multilingual demos, disclosed in the worker header.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = translation_text. worker.js returns {translation, pair, inTokens, outTokens, beams, ms} — real per-pair input_ids length + streamed output. It exposes en-de/en-fr/en-es plus the reverse pairs (de-en/fr-en/es-en) so the round-trip (wild) demo stays entirely Marian. io-shape-semantic conformance assertion covers a real translation for the selected pair.
rubric.notes: Round-trip back-translation is possible precisely because both directions are available as separate small models.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares Xenova/opus-mt-en-de (primary, linked, 5 occurrences) plus en-fr / en-es, the translation task, q8 dtype, WASM backend, ~105 MB per pair and CC-BY-4.0 license — matching models.json (hfId Xenova/opus-mt-en-de). declares-model-id/task/backend/quantisation conformance assertions tested pass.
rubric.notes: Each pair is ~105 MB; switching pairs downloads only the new one. Inference off the main thread.

rubric.evidence: The page uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init for the default pair (Xenova/opus-mt-en-de, q8, ~105 MB, requiresWebGPU:false); a valid current cached copy auto-initialises with no Load click. Additional pairs load on demand via the worker's ensure/pair-loading/pair-ready messages. shared-auto-init-loader conformance assertion tested pass.
rubric.notes: HONEST NUANCE: the shared auto-init loader tracks the DEFAULT pair's cache; when the user switches to another pair, that pair downloads via the worker's own pair-loading path (with pair-ready confirmation) rather than the shared loader's UI. Each pair is small (~105 MB) so this is low-cost, but per-pair cache state is not surfaced through the same Download/Update controls as the primary — a disclosed design nuance.

rubric.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live; the worker posts pair-loading/pair-ready + progress{pair} so pair switches announce state; streamed partial translation in an aria-live region; worker errors (incl. 'Unknown language pair') surface on the page; shared loader adds checking→initialising→ready + Retry. live-status-region + errors-on-the-page conformance assertions tested pass; honest-progress-retry-offline / runs-without-webgpu-fallback manual-evidenced.

Real, labelled controls: the source text <textarea>, a language-pair <select>, and a translate button (plus the round-trip wild control). All keyboard-operable. controls-operable conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The pair <select> is the defining control — choosing a pair selects a different bilingual model, the architectural point of the demo.

rubric.evidence: No canned translation: streamed text and token counts are from the real per-pair pipeline. no-fake-output conformance assertion is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Round-trip results are two real Marian passes, not a scripted echo.

rubric.evidence: translate button + pair select carry names (buttons-have-names tested pass); status role=status aria-live; output region aria-live; select labelled; page lang=en; WCAG-AA light+dark. images-have-alt tested pass.
rubric.notes: As with the other translation demos, the output region uses page lang=en rather than a dynamic target-language lang — open item.

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop/responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) conformance assertions present; design-system panels. models.json support is needs-review on both classes (automated pre-scan flagged the MOST sub-24px targets in the batch: 13 desktop / 9 mobile), pending an agent matrix pass to confirm tap sizes and flip to ok.
rubric.notes: 13/9 flagged sub-24px targets is the highest count in this batch — a real responsive item to address, recorded honestly as needs-review.

rubric.evidence: Translation runs in the Web Worker; tokens stream to the main thread. inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: The small per-pair models decode quickly; work is off-thread + streamed, so INP stays low.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move heavy work off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run generation off the main thread and stream progress; keep INP low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — per-pair translation runs in the worker; TextStreamer streams the decode.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread streaming; inference-off-main-thread conformance assertion tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form labels select menu text input
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control including the <select>; keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the language-pair select and source textarea are labelled and keyboard-operable.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/pair-switch/ready via role=status aria-live; give controls names.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — runStatus role=status aria-live; the worker's pair-loading/pair-ready messages drive the status; translate/pair controls named.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: live-status-region + buttons-have-names conformance assertions tested pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with wrapping; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — design-system panels; controls flex-wrap. Note the high sub-24px tap-target flag count is the outstanding responsive item.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive assertions present; support needs-review (13/9 sub-24px flags) pending agent matrix pass

openQuestions: This page has the highest sub-24px tap-target flag count in the batch (13 desktop / 9 mobile) — which controls (pair select, round-trip controls) need a min-height bump to clear ≈44px?
Should per-pair cache state (downloaded / absent for each pair) be surfaced through the shared loader's Download/Update UI, rather than only the worker's pair-loading messages?

followUpGoals.goal: Run the mobile+desktop responsive matrix, raise the flagged sub-24px tap targets (13 desktop / 9 mobile — the highest in the batch), and flip models.json support to ok.
Surface per-pair cache/download state (each ~105 MB pair) in the loader UI so switching pairs shows an explicit download control consistent with the primary-pair auto-init.

summary: Retrospective, code-grounded revision-1 critique for marianmt-translation (Xenova/opus-mt-en-de + en-fr/en-es + reverse pairs, translation, q8, ~105 MB/pair, CC-BY-4.0). Real BILINGUAL translation: worker.js holds a pair→pipeline Map and loads each small OPUS-MT model on demand (no src_lang/tgt_lang args — the pair IS the model), streams tokens via TextStreamer, and exposes reverse pairs for an all-Marian round-trip. Default-pair auto-init via createModelLoader; role=status aria-live states with pair-loading/pair-ready announcements; no fake output. Verified against the immutable conformance suite (calls-real-inference, loads-clean, declares-*, inference-off-main-thread, shared-auto-init-loader, live-status-region, buttons-have-names tested pass; io-shape/parity/inp manual-evidenced). No fabricated numbers. Honest weaknesses flagged: HIGHEST sub-24px tap-target flag count in the batch (13 desktop / 9 mobile) — support needs-review, priority high; per-pair cache state isn't surfaced through the shared loader's Download/Update UI (only the worker's pair-loading messages); output region lacks a dynamic target-language lang.
